name: radar
description: Native radar / spider chart — items rated across multiple axes.
purpose: Use to compare 2–4 options across the same 4–8 criteria. Each option becomes a polygon; overlap shows where strengths align.

series.description: One li per series (option). Format: `Label — v1, v2, v3, v4, …` one number per axis.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: radar -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## How we stack up across the buying criteria.

- Lattice
  - Performance `9`
  - Pricing `7`
  - Support `8`
  - Ecosystem `6`
  - Security `9`
- Rival North
  - Performance `7`
  - Pricing `8`
  - Support `6`
  - Ecosystem `9`
  - Security `7`
- Rival West
  - Performance `6`
  - Pricing `9`
  - Support `7`
  - Ecosystem `8`
  - Security `8`
sample:
<!-- _class: radar -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## How we stack up across the buying criteria.

- Meridian
  - Performance `9`
  - Pricing `7`
  - Support `8`
  - Ecosystem `6`
  - Security `9`
- Vantage
  - Performance `7`
  - Pricing `8`
  - Support `6`
  - Ecosystem `9`
  - Security `7`
- Helios
  - Performance `6`
  - Pricing `9`
  - Support `7`
  - Ecosystem `8`
  - Security `8`

whenToUse.title: Same criteria, multiple options
whenToUse.body: Competitive comparison, vendor evaluation, candidate scoring — anywhere two to four options need to be rated on the same four-to-eight criteria. The polygon shapes show the trade-off pattern at a glance.

whenToUse.title: Shape is the argument
whenToUse.body: Radar charts are good at 'we are strong here and weak there' — the lopsided polygon is the message. If precise pairwise comparisons matter more than the silhouette, use a grouped bar chart or `verdict-grid`.

whenToUse.title: Shared zero-to-ten scale
whenToUse.body: Every axis must use the same scale so the polygons are comparable. Mixed units (ms, $, count) collapse the chart's meaning — normalise to a shared 0–10 or 0–100 before authoring.

antiPatterns.title: More than four series
antiPatterns.body: Five overlapping polygons become a tangle of edges. Trim to the two or three options the audience is actually choosing between; the rest belong in an appendix table.

antiPatterns.title: Three or fewer axes
antiPatterns.body: Three axes makes a triangle — barely a shape. Below four criteria, the spider collapses and the slide should be a `cards-grid` or `verdict-grid` instead.

antiPatterns.title: Mixed scales across axes
antiPatterns.body: If one axis is 0–10 and another is 0–10,000, the larger axis dominates the polygon and the comparison is misleading. Normalise everything to a shared scale first.

related.name: quadrant
related.when: two axes are enough — the other six dimensions drop out

related.name: verdict-grid
related.when: the criteria are categorical (pass/fail), not graded

related.name: kpi
related.when: the comparison is one option's metrics, not multi-option

related.name: compare-table
related.when: a precise tabular comparison reads better than a shape

related.name: piechart
related.when: the question is part-to-whole, not multi-criterion

cover.label: Cover — full-bleed with a caption band
cover.caption: The chart-family `cover` modifier: the diagram fills the whole slide and the title + a one-line takeaway reflow into a bottom caption band carrying the chart sheen. For a hero comparison slide where the radar IS the message. Chart-scoped — not an all-layout modifier.
cover.sample:
<!-- _class: radar cover -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## How we stack up across the buying criteria.

- Meridian
  - Performance `9`
  - Pricing `7`
  - Support `8`
  - Ecosystem `6`
  - Security `9`
- Vantage
  - Performance `7`
  - Pricing `8`
  - Support `6`
  - Ecosystem `9`
  - Security `7`
- Helios
  - Performance `6`
  - Pricing `9`
  - Support `7`
  - Ecosystem `8`
  - Security `8`

Meridian leads on performance and support; Helios is the value pick across pricing and ecosystem.

target.label: Target — actual vs reference ring
target.caption: Reads the series named `Target` (or `Goal`/`Plan`) as a dashed reference polygon; each axis where the actual falls short gets a rose gap segment along the spoke, surplus a quiet green one. The shortfall is the read.
target.sample:
<!-- _class: radar target -->

`Scale · 0–100`

## Where we are against the quarter plan.

- Actual
  - Hiring `72`
  - Runway `88`
  - Pipeline `54`
  - Retention `91`
  - Compliance `66`
  - Velocity `78`
- Target
  - Hiring `90`
  - Runway `85`
  - Pipeline `80`
  - Retention `90`
  - Compliance `95`
  - Velocity `75`

delta.label: Delta — before vs after
delta.caption: Takes exactly two series — before, then after. The before polygon is muted; a change segment rides each spoke, green where the metric rose, rose where it fell. Built for the period-over-period QBR story.
delta.sample:
<!-- _class: radar delta -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## What moved over the half, and which way.

- H1
  - Velocity `5`
  - Quality `6`
  - Morale `4`
  - Coverage `5`
  - Onboarding `3`
- H2
  - Velocity `8`
  - Quality `7`
  - Morale `4`
  - Coverage `6`
  - Onboarding `7`

benchmark.label: Benchmark — hero vs envelope
benchmark.caption: Draws the first series as the hero line and collapses every other series into a single min–max envelope band — one shape and your line instead of five tangled polygons. Strong where you clear the band, exposed where you sit inside it.
benchmark.sample:
<!-- _class: radar benchmark -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## Are we inside the pack, or outside it.

- Us
  - Performance `9`
  - Price `6`
  - Support `8`
  - Ecosystem `7`
  - Docs `9`
  - Security `8`
- Competitor A
  - Performance `7`
  - Price `8`
  - Support `6`
  - Ecosystem `9`
  - Docs `5`
  - Security `7`
- Competitor B
  - Performance `6`
  - Price `9`
  - Support `7`
  - Ecosystem `6`
  - Docs `6`
  - Security `6`
- Competitor C
  - Performance `8`
  - Price `5`
  - Support `5`
  - Ecosystem `8`
  - Docs `7`
  - Security `9`

quadrant.label: Quadrant — axes grouped into sectors
quadrant.caption: Takes a three-level list (series → group → axis); each group becomes a tinted sector with its name on the rim and a dashed mean arc. For boards that think in themes, not twelve loose axes.
quadrant.sample:
<!-- _class: radar quadrant -->

`Scale · 0–5`

## Our capability profile, read by theme.

- Our capability
  - People
    - Hiring `4`
    - Retention `3`
    - Bench depth `2`
  - Process
    - Cadence `5`
    - Rigor `4`
  - Technology
    - Platform `4`
    - Tooling `3`
    - Automation `2`
  - Risk
    - Compliance `3`
    - Resilience `4`

small-multiples.label: Small-multiples — one mini radar per series
small-multiples.caption: Gives each series its own mini radar on a shared scale, laid out in a row — the honest read when an overlay of four-plus series would be mush. Scan the shapes, spot the outlier.
small-multiples.sample:
<!-- _class: radar small-multiples -->

`Scale · 0–10`

## Four product lines, the same five criteria.

- Atlas
  - Adoption `8`
  - Margin `6`
  - NPS `7`
  - Velocity `9`
  - Risk `4`
- Beacon
  - Adoption `5`
  - Margin `9`
  - NPS `6`
  - Velocity `5`
  - Risk `7`
- Cinder
  - Adoption `7`
  - Margin `4`
  - NPS `8`
  - Velocity `6`
  - Risk `5`
- Drift
  - Adoption `6`
  - Margin `7`
  - NPS `5`
  - Velocity `7`
  - Risk `8`

minimal.label: Minimal — stroke-only
minimal.caption: Composable modifier: drops the polygon fills for a stroke-only read on a faint grid. Layers on the default or any radar variant when the fills would muddy the comparison.
minimal.sample:

  - Security `7`
